identity.description: A jazz-literate music agent that writes scores, comps over changes, and renders real audio. Ask for a backing track on a standard, a walking bassline through ii–V–I, or a transcription — it produces a MusicXML/MIDI artifact and a rendered take, not just prose.

inputs.description: Routed call to a jazz-guru capability. Most callers use `skill: "chat"` to converse — the agent will compose, arrange, render, transcribe, and analyse as needed. `render_midi` is a direct utility for rendering an existing MIDI file with an instrument preset.

example.message: Give me a swung 16-bar backing track for 'Autumn Leaves' in E minor — piano + walking upright bass, medium tempo. Render an MP3.

message.title: Message (chat)
message.description: User message for the chat skill.

session_id.title: Session ID
session_id.description: Existing jazz-guru session UUID. Optional for chat/render_midi (auto-created if absent), required for distill.

title.title: Session title (chat)
title.description: Optional title when chat auto-creates a session.

only.title: Eval task filter (evalrun)
only.description: Run a single eval task by id; omit to run the full regression suite.

midi_path.title: MIDI path (render_midi)
midi_path.description: MIDI file path relative to the session workspace.

out_path.title: Output path (render_midi)
out_path.description: Output audio path (.wav / .flac / .mp3) relative to the session workspace.

post_process.title: Post-processing chain (render_midi)
post_process.description: ffmpeg filter overrides; merged on top of preset defaults.

outputs.description: Skill-specific JSON envelope. `chat` returns `session_id` + `text` + `usage` + the artifact paths the agent produced. `render_midi` returns `result.path` for the rendered audio.

skills.name: Make music with you
skills.description: Open-ended musical conversation. Ask for a backing track over a standard, a comp through a tricky changes section, a melody in a particular voice, a transcription of an audio clip, or a discussion of a player's vocabulary. The agent produces playable artifacts (MusicXML, MIDI, rendered audio) and explains its choices in concise prose — voice-leading, harmonic function, rhythmic feel.
examples: {"skill":"chat","message":"Backing track for 'There Will Never Be Another You' in Ab, medium swing, 2 choruses. Piano comping + walking upright bass. Render as MP3."}
{"skill":"chat","message":"Comp 8 bars over | Dm7 | G7 | Cmaj7 | A7 | Dm7 | G7 | Cmaj7 | Cmaj7 | in a Bill Evans rootless-voicing style. Render with the Salamander piano."}
{"skill":"chat","message":"Find five Coltrane licks from the Weimar Jazz Database that move from a ii7 to a V7alt, and transcribe the best one as MusicXML."}

skills.name: Write a score
skills.description: Ask for notation. Lead sheets, comping voicings, melodies, basslines, drum charts — the agent generates MusicXML you can open in MuseScore / Sibelius / Dorico, and can render the same material to audio in the same turn. Hand it a key, tempo, form, and a stylistic direction; it will ship a draft and surface any assumptions it had to make.
examples: {"skill":"score","message":"Write a 32-bar lead sheet (AABA) for an original ballad in F minor in the vein of 'My One and Only Love'. Output MusicXML and a piano-only audio preview."}
{"skill":"score","message":"Transcribe this melody by ear-reasoning over the changes, then notate it as MusicXML in concert pitch and a Bb-transposed part for tenor sax."}

{"skill":"score","message":"Generate a walking bass part through the first chorus of 'Stella by Starlight' in Bb, four-to-the-floor, with chromatic approach notes on beat 4 where appropriate. Notate and render."}

skills.name: Render MIDI to audio
skills.description: Direct utility: hand it a MIDI file and a preset, get an audio file back. Presets cover General MIDI (FluidSynth) plus sampled real instruments — MTG solo saxes (tenor / alto / soprano / baritone), Salamander Grand Piano, Smolken pizzicato upright bass. Optional ffmpeg post-processing chain (lowpass, vibrato, loudness normalization) is wired in per preset and overridable per call.
